skill.name: react-patterns
skill.description: Modern React patterns and principles. Hooks, composition, performance, TypeScript best practices.
skill.summary: A comprehensive guide to production-ready React development including hooks, state management, and TypeScript patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 6 static analyzer findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is educational documentation about React patterns, not executable code. Pattern matches on words like 'useLocalStorage' and section headings triggered false alarms. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Master React development patterns and best practices
content.value_statement: Build production-ready React applications with confidence. Learn proven patterns for hooks, state management, and component design.
actual_capabilities: Explains component types: server, client, presentational, and container components
Provides hook extraction patterns for localStorage, debounce, fetch, and forms
Guides state management selection from useState to global stores
Covers React 19 hooks including useActionState and useOptimistic
Teaches composition patterns like compound components and render props
Explains TypeScript typing for props, events, and refs
limitations: Documentation-only skill without code execution capabilities
Does not provide project-specific implementation or scaffolding
Requires existing React project context for practical application
Does not replace official React documentation or framework guides

use_cases.title: React Developer Onboarding
use_cases.description: Help new team members learn your React architecture patterns and coding standards quickly.

use_cases.title: Component Refactoring Guidance
use_cases.description: Get recommendations for breaking down large components and applying proper hook patterns.

use_cases.title: State Management Decisions
use_cases.description: Determine the right state solution from useState to global stores based on your needs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Hook Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain when to extract a custom hook in React. Show the useLocalStorage pattern with example code.

prompt_templates.title: Component Design Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component and suggest improvements based on component design principles. Identify if it should be split into smaller components.

prompt_templates.title: State Management Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an app with user auth, theme preferences, and real-time notifications. Recommend a state management approach and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: React 19 Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how React 19 compiler affects memoization patterns. Show which useMemo and useCallback calls can be removed.

output_examples.input: When should I use Context versus a global store like Zustand?
output_examples.output: Use Context for local shared state within a component subtree like theme or locale. Use Zustand for app-wide state that needs devtools, persistence, or is accessed across many unrelated components. Context is built-in but can cause re-renders; Zustand offers better performance for frequent updates.

output_examples.input: Is this useEffect necessary for fetching data on mount?
output_examples.output: In React 19, prefer server components or data-fetching libraries like React Query over useEffect for data fetching. useEffect is appropriate for subscriptions or non-data side effects. For data, use libraries that handle caching, deduplication, and error states automatically.

best_practices: Profile performance before optimizing - measure actual bottlenecks first
Prefer composition over inheritance - build small, focused components
Use stable unique keys instead of array indices for list rendering
anti_patterns: Deep prop drilling - use Context or component composition instead
Giant components with multiple responsibilities - split into smaller units
Using useEffect for everything - leverage server components where appropriate

faq.question: When should I extract a custom hook?
faq.answer: Extract a custom hook when the same logic pattern appears in multiple components, when you need to reuse stateful logic, or when component code becomes hard to read due to hook complexity.

faq.question: Should I use Redux or Context for my app?
faq.answer: Start with Context for simple shared state. Use Redux or Zustand when you need devtools, middleware, time-travel debugging, or have complex state interactions across many components.

faq.question: Are React 19 compiler features backward compatible?
faq.answer: React 19 requires React 19+. The compiler automates memoization but your existing useMemo and useCallback calls remain valid. You can remove unnecessary ones after upgrading.

faq.question: How do I choose between useState and useReducer?
faq.answer: Use useState for simple values and independent state. Use useReducer when state has complex transitions, when multiple values update together, or when state logic benefits from explicit action types.

faq.question: What is the compound component pattern?
faq.answer: Compound components work together through shared context. A parent component provides state via Context, and child components consume it. Examples include Tabs with TabList/Tab/TabPanel or Select with Option components.

faq.question: When should I use error boundaries?
faq.answer: Place error boundaries around features that should fail independently. Use app-wide boundaries for graceful degradation, feature-level for isolated failures, and component-level for risky third-party integrations.
